overview.location: East Boston and Winthrop, Massachusetts — 3 miles northeast of downtown Boston
overview.distinction: New England's primary airport and the 16th busiest in the United States. Opened September 8, 1923 and named after Spanish-American War veteran General Edward Lawrence Logan. Famously home to the FIRST airport chapel in the United States (Our Lady of the Airways, opened 1951) and the FIRST U.S. airport with direct rapid transit connectivity (Blue Line, 1952). Terminal A was the FIRST LEED-certified airport terminal in the U.S. (2005). Major Delta Air Lines secondary transatlantic hub and JetBlue Airways' largest focus city.
overview.namedAfter: General Edward Lawrence Logan, Spanish-American War officer
overview.metroAccess: Silver Line SL1 bus picks up at every terminal curb — FREE from airport to South Station in downtown Boston. Blue Line Airport Station accessible via free Massport shuttle.
overview.firstsAndRecords: First airport chapel in the U.S. (1951). First U.S. airport with rapid transit connection (1952). First LEED-certified airport terminal (Terminal A, 2005).
overview.surroundingNote: Located on a peninsula in Boston Harbor. Only major U.S. airport completely surrounded by water on three sides. Close proximity to downtown Boston (3 miles) is uncommon for a major international airport.

terminals.name: Terminal A
terminals.status: Active — 21 gates. First LEED-certified airport terminal in the U.S. (2005).
terminals.notes: Delta Air Lines hub terminal. Terminal A is connected to the satellite building via an underground pedestrian tunnel with moving walkways. Home to Delta Sky Club (near Gate A18) and the new Delta One Lounge. Delta check-in at desks A1-A5. Terminal A-B connector under construction (approved May 2024) will eventually link Terminal A to Terminal B post-security.

terminals.name: Terminal B
terminals.status: Active — 41 gates. Largest terminal at Logan by gate count. Currently undergoing multi-year renovation & expansion.
terminals.notes: Terminal B is split into two piers (North and South) which are not connected post-security. American Airlines operates from B201-B206 (west side). Southwest check-in at B17-B19, gates B20-B32. United check-in at B11-B15. Alaska/Spirit/Southwest/Boutique at gates B29-B40. Home to American Airlines Admirals Club (upper level near B4). Terminal B has its own dedicated parking garage separate from Central Parking.

terminals.name: Terminal C
terminals.status: Active — 27 gates. JetBlue Airways' largest operating base nationwide. Currently being upgraded (2026) with larger Gate C10 hold room, modernized security checkpoint, and refreshed restrooms.
terminals.notes: JetBlue's largest focus city globally. JetBlue check-in at desks C1-C10. JetBlue 'BlueHouse BOS' lounge opening 2026 near Gate C23 (11,000 sq ft — 40% larger than JetBlue's JFK lounge). United Club located in Terminal C. American Express Centurion Lounge announced for 2029 (two-story lounge with terrace). New Kidport opening early 2026.

terminals.name: Terminal E
terminals.status: Active — 18 gates. Dedicated international terminal. Completed $680M expansion August 2023 (320,000 sq ft addition with 4 new gates and distinctive red roof).
terminals.notes: All international arrivals processed through Terminal E customs and immigration. Houses British Airways Galleries Lounge (4th floor opposite Gate E11), Emirates Lounge (opposite E11, daily 7:05 PM-10:05 PM), Lufthansa Lounge (across from E11, daily 12:15 PM-9:30 PM), Air France Lounge, and Delta Sky Club (new 21,000 sq ft lounge for international departures, seats 400+). Terminal E Lot parking currently closed. New 5,000-space Terminal E parking garage under construction (~2028 completion) with solar panels on facade and roof.

roads: Interstate 90 (Massachusetts Turnpike — via Ted Williams Tunnel)
transit: MBTA Silver Line SL1 (bus — FREE from airport, direct to South Station)
MBTA Blue Line via Airport Station (accessible by free Massport shuttle)
Logan Express (dedicated bus from 5 suburban stations)
MBTA Commuter Rail (via South Station transfer)

silverLine.fare: FREE from airport to downtown Boston. Return trips from South Station to airport: $2.40 with CharlieCard, $2.90 cash
silverLine.pickupLocation: Every terminal curbside — look for 'Silver Line' signs on the departures/arrivals level
silverLine.hours: Approximately 5:00 AM - 1:00 AM daily (check MBTA for exact times)
silverLine.frequency: Every 6-15 minutes depending on time of day

travelTimes.toBackBay: ~20-25 minutes (via South Station + Orange Line transfer)

silverLine.connections: Free transfer at South Station to Red Line (toward Cambridge/Harvard/MIT) and Commuter Rail. Free transfer at Silver Line Way.
silverLine.competitorNote: The Silver Line SL1 is one of the best airport-to-downtown transit deals in America. FREE from airport direct to South Station in 15-17 minutes. No other major U.S. airport offers free transit to downtown. For anyone headed to downtown Boston, Seaport, Cambridge (via Red Line transfer at South Station), or the Financial District, the Silver Line beats both parking and rideshare on cost — though it's not the fastest during peak traffic.

blueLine.accessFromTerminals: Take FREE Massport On-Airport Shuttle (Routes 22, 33, 55, or 66) from any terminal to Airport Station — runs every ~15 minutes
blueLine.hours: Approximately 5:15 AM - 12:45 AM Sun-Thu, 5:15 AM - 1:15 AM Fri-Sat

travelTimes.toBackBay: ~20-25 minutes (via transfer to Orange Line at State)
travelTimes.toFenwayPark: ~25-30 minutes (via Green Line transfer at Government Center)

blueLine.connections: Orange Line at State Street, Green Line at Government Center, Commuter Rail at North Station (2 stops north)
blueLine.note: Blue Line is the slightly longer route vs Silver Line but provides direct subway access to Downtown Crossing, Government Center, North Station, and Wonderland (Revere Beach).

loganExpress.description: Dedicated bus service from 5 suburban stations directly to all Logan terminals (lower level)

stations.name: Back Bay
stations.location: Hynes Convention Center area, downtown Boston

loganExpress.fare: $9 each way online (limited-time promotional pricing). Back Bay: $3 to airport with FREE return. Kids ride free with a ticketed family member.
loganExpress.frequency: Every 20-30 minutes at most stations during peak hours
loganExpress.travelTime: 30-60 minutes depending on station and traffic
loganExpress.note: Logan Express combines free/cheap suburban parking with dedicated bus service — ideal for travelers from suburbs who want to avoid driving into the city or parking at the airport. At $7/day parking + $9/each way, a 5-day trip costs ~$53 total vs $185-230 for Central Parking.

taxi.pickup: Designated taxi stands at every terminal, arrivals level
taxi.loganAirportFee: $2.75 airport fee added to all taxi trips originating at Logan

estimatedFares.toDowntownBoston: $25-$45 (depending on time/traffic, excluding tip and tolls)

taxi.tolls: Ted Williams Tunnel or Sumner Tunnel tolls may apply for trips to/from downtown (~$2-$3)
taxi.note: Boston taxis are metered and typically cost more than rideshare. All major credit cards accepted. 24/7 service.

rideshare.pickupLocation: Designated Ride App Pickup zones at each terminal — slightly farther from central taxi stand. Look for signage for Uber/Lyft pickup after getting baggage.
rideshare.airportFee: $5.50 per trip (increased from $3.25 effective July 2024) — passed through to rider via Massport agreement
rideshare.surgePricing: Standard surge pricing during peak hours, weather events, holidays, and Red Sox/Bruins/Celtics game days
rideshare.competitiveAdvantage: Rideshare from BOS is one of the cheapest among major U.S. airports because downtown Boston is only 3 miles away. A $20-25 UberX to downtown is competitive with the $2.40 Blue Line if you have luggage and limited time. However, the FREE Silver Line SL1 to South Station beats rideshare on pure cost for budget travelers.

rentalCars.location: Consolidated Rental Car Center (RCC) — 15 Transportation Way, East Boston, MA 02128
rentalCars.accessFromTerminals: FREE Massport On-Airport Shuttle (Routes 22, 33, or 55) — runs every 5-6 minutes
rentalCars.facility: Four-level consolidated facility housing all major rental agencies under one roof — opened to centralize rental operations and free up terminal curb space
rentalCars.note: All rental car companies are located off-airport at the RCC. Return your rental to the RCC and take the free shuttle back to your terminal. Shuttles run approximately every 5-6 minutes, 24/7.

waterTransportation.location: Water Transportation Dock, accessed via free Massport On-Airport Shuttle Route 66 (every ~15 minutes)

services.name: MBTA Hingham/Hull Ferry
services.route: Logan Airport → Long Wharf (downtown Boston) → Hull → Hingham

services.name: Boston Water Taxi
services.coverage: Downtown Boston, Charlestown, East Boston, Seaport District

waterTransportation.note: Water transportation is a unique BOS feature — one of the only U.S. airports where you can leave by boat. Great for travelers heading to Seaport District hotels, downtown waterfront, or harbor-area destinations. The 7-minute water taxi from airport to downtown beats any ground transportation during rush hour.

massportShuttle.description: FREE on-airport shuttle connecting all terminals to parking facilities, Rental Car Center, Airport Blue Line Station, and Water Transportation Dock

routes.route22: Terminals → Airport Station (Blue Line) → RCC → Water Taxi Dock
routes.route33: Terminals → Airport Station → RCC → Water Taxi Dock
routes.route55: Terminals → Airport Station → RCC → Water Taxi Dock
routes.route66: Terminals → Airport Station → Water Transportation Dock

parkingRates: On-airport: Central Parking Garage $46/day (up to $42/day with advance reservation at $29/day), Terminal B Garage similar rates, Economy Parking Garage $37/day. Advance reservations limited at $29/day. Off-site alternatives from ~$12-20/day with free shuttle. Pre-booking recommended — Terminal E lots currently closed during construction.

centralParkingGarage.name: Central Parking Garage
centralParkingGarage.connection: Direct connection to Terminals A, B, C, and E via pedestrian bridges on Level 4
centralParkingGarage.evCharging: 38 EV charging ports (Level 6: 18 ports, Level 6 West: 20 ports)
centralParkingGarage.features: Closest parking to all terminals. Short-term and long-term parking available. 24/7 attendants. Complimentary jump starts, lockout assistance, tire service. FlyLogan app 'Mark My Car' feature.

terminalBGarage.name: Terminal B Parking Garage
terminalBGarage.rate: Same rate structure as Central Parking
terminalBGarage.features: Convenient for American, United, Southwest, Alaska, Spirit passengers flying from Terminal B
terminalBGarage.note: Lower height clearance (6'6") — oversized vehicles should use Central Parking instead

economyParkingGarage.name: Economy Parking Garage
economyParkingGarage.connection: FREE shuttle bus to all terminals (A, B, C, E) — runs every 15-20 minutes
economyParkingGarage.features: Budget-friendly option. 24/7 attendants. All major credit cards and cash accepted. Head-in parking encouraged.

terminalELots.name: Terminal E Surface Lots
terminalELots.reason: Under construction for new 5,000-space Terminal E parking garage (completion ~2028). Features solar panels on facade and roof.
terminalELots.alternative: Use Central Parking Garage (connects to Terminal E via Level 4 pedestrian bridge) or Economy Parking (free shuttle)

cellPhoneLot.name: Cell Phone Waiting Lot
cellPhoneLot.location: Intersection of Hotel Drive and Service Road, East Boston, MA 02128
cellPhoneLot.directions: Follow 'Cell Phone Lot' signs from the inbound roadway. Near Airport MBTA Station and Shell gas station.
cellPhoneLot.rules: Vehicles must not be left unattended. No restroom facilities. Max 30 minutes stay. Massport occasionally opens overflow lot near Terminal E during holidays/peak periods.
cellPhoneLot.note: Popular during holiday weekends, summer vacation, major Boston events (Red Sox home games, Marathon, Fourth of July). Arrive 10-15 minutes after passenger lands, not before.

valetParking.status: NOT AVAILABLE — Logan Airport does not offer on-site valet parking

accessibleParking.note: Accessible parking is guaranteed in all open garages for vehicles with valid disability plates or placards, EVEN when the garage appears full. Ask any parking attendant for assistance.

onAirportParking.totalCapacity: Approximately 18,000 vehicles across all Logan Airport parking facilities
onAirportParking.totalEVSpaces: 173 hybrid, electric, and alternative-fuel-only parking spaces across all facilities
onAirportParking.paymentMethods: Cash, all major credit cards accepted. Advance reservations available online at massport.com.
onAirportParking.parkingAssistance: 24/7 call boxes in all garages for jump starts, lockouts, tire service (complimentary)

parkingLots.name: Park Shuttle & Fly
parkingLots.shuttleInfo: FREE 24/7 on-demand shuttle — 5-10 minute ride to terminal. Family-owned and operated since 1975.
parkingLots.overview: The closest off-site parking lot to Boston Logan Airport — the only off-site lot on this side of the drawbridge. Family-owned since 1975. First and longest-operating off-site parking facility at Logan. Save up to 40% vs airport garages.
parkingLots.address: 320 William F McClellan Hwy, Boston, MA 02128
knownCoupons: Frequent Parker Rewards Program: $2/day discount for members
parkingLots.notes: Closest off-site lot. On-demand shuttle means no waiting. Best for travelers who want savings without giving up convenience.

parkingLots.name: Park N Boston
parkingLots.shuttleInfo: Free shuttle runs every 30 minutes. ~8 minute ride to terminals.
parkingLots.overview: Affordable off-site parking 6 minutes from Logan Airport. Rewards members receive one FREE day plus 10% off Standard & Oversized rates. Points redeemable toward free stays, car wash, and other perks.
parkingLots.notes: Strong rewards program makes this cheapest for repeat travelers. Shuttle runs every 30 minutes, not on-demand.

parkingLots.name: Preflight Airport Parking Boston
parkingLots.shuttleInfo: Free shuttle every 7-10 minutes with real-time tracking. Frequent service compared to hotel lots.
parkingLots.overview: National parking brand with a Boston location serving Logan. Real-time shuttle tracking via their app. Loyalty program offers free parking days and ongoing discounts/promotions throughout the year.
parkingLots.notes: Reliable nationwide brand. Real-time shuttle tracking is a nice feature. Slightly more expensive than Park Shuttle & Fly but more amenities.

parkingLots.name: Hilton Boston Logan Airport
parkingLots.shuttleInfo: No shuttle needed — hotel is connected to Logan via Massport shuttle or walking distance to Terminal A
parkingLots.overview: The only hotel directly on Logan Airport property. Connected via sky bridge to Terminal A and Terminal E. No shuttle required — walk or take the free Massport shuttle. Premium option for early morning or late night flights.
parkingLots.notes: The closest hotel to the airport. Higher rate but unmatched convenience. Popular Park-Sleep-Fly option for travelers with very early morning flights.

parkingLots.name: Hyatt Regency Boston Harbor
parkingLots.shuttleInfo: Complimentary shuttle to Logan terminals — runs every 20-30 minutes
parkingLots.overview: Waterfront hotel with free airport shuttle. Located on Boston Harbor with panoramic views. Park-Sleep-Fly packages available. Good balance between airport access and quality stay.
parkingLots.notes: Best for travelers who want a nice hotel stay + parking. Harbor views are a differentiator. Shuttle on schedule (not on-demand).

parkingLots.name: Embassy Suites by Hilton Boston at Logan Airport
parkingLots.overview: All-suite hotel close to Logan with free breakfast and evening reception included. 24-hour airport shuttle. Good value for families needing separate living space plus parking.
knownCoupons: Park-Sleep-Fly packages include up to 14 days parking
parkingLots.notes: 24-hour shuttle is a major advantage over most competitors. Free breakfast + evening reception adds significant value. Best for families or longer stays.

parkingLots.name: Courtyard by Marriott Boston Logan Airport
parkingLots.overview: Modern Marriott-brand hotel near Logan with airport shuttle. Good middle-tier park-sleep-fly option.
parkingLots.address: 225 William F McClellan Hwy, Boston, MA 02128
parkingLots.notes: Marriott brand reliability. Shuttle runs on schedule (not 24/7 on-demand). Reasonable rates.

parkingLots.name: Holiday Inn Express Boston Logan Airport
parkingLots.shuttleInfo: Complimentary airport shuttle — limited hours, check schedule
parkingLots.overview: Budget-friendly park-sleep-fly option. Free breakfast included. Good for early flights and simple packages.
parkingLots.notes: Budget option. Shuttle not 24/7 — verify hours for early/late flights. Free breakfast is a plus.

parkingLots.name: Homewood Suites by Hilton Boston Logan Airport Chelsea
parkingLots.overview: Extended-stay hotel 12 minutes from Logan with 24-hour shuttle service and free hot breakfast. Kitchenettes in all rooms. Good for longer trips.
parkingLots.notes: 24-hour shuttle is a major advantage. Kitchenettes + free breakfast + longer stays make this great for extended trips. Slightly farther drive but good value.

shuttleInfo: On-airport: FREE 24/7 Massport shuttles connect all terminals to parking, Rental Car Center, Airport Blue Line Station, and Water Taxi Dock (Routes 11/22/33/55/66/88, every 5-15 min). Off-site: Most hotel and off-site lots provide free shuttle service — frequency varies from on-demand (Park Shuttle & Fly) to every 30 min. NOTE: Some hotel shuttles are NOT 24/7 — verify hours for early/late flights.
distanceFromCity: 3 miles northeast of downtown Boston. 10-25 minutes by car via Ted Williams Tunnel or Sumner Tunnel. 15-17 minutes to South Station by FREE Silver Line SL1. Logan is one of the closest major international airports to a downtown core in the U.S.

keyDistances.toDowntownBoston: 3 miles, 10-25 min by car, 15 min by Silver Line (FREE), $20-35 rideshare
keyDistances.toBackBay: 4 miles, 15-30 min by car, 25 min by Silver Line + Orange Line transfer, $25-40 rideshare
keyDistances.toSouthStation: 3 miles, 10-25 min by car, 15 min by Silver Line (FREE), $25-35 rideshare
keyDistances.toCambridge: 5 miles, 15-30 min by car, 25-30 min by Silver Line + Red Line transfer, $25-40 rideshare
keyDistances.toHarvardSquare: 7 miles, 20-35 min by car, 30-35 min by public transit, $35-50 rideshare
keyDistances.toMITKendall: 5 miles, 15-30 min by car, 25 min by public transit, $25-40 rideshare
keyDistances.toFenwayPark: 5 miles, 20-35 min by car, 30 min by public transit, $25-40 rideshare
keyDistances.toSeaportDistrict: 2 miles, 8-15 min by car, 8 min by Silver Line, $15-25 rideshare
keyDistances.toQuincyMarket: 3 miles, 10-20 min by car, 20 min by transit, $20-35 rideshare
keyDistances.toNYC: 220 miles, 4 hours by car, 3.5 hours by Amtrak Acela from South Station
keyDistances.competitiveAdvantage: BOS's proximity to downtown Boston (3 miles) is exceptional for a major international airport. Combined with the FREE Silver Line SL1 to South Station, Logan offers one of the best airport-to-downtown experiences in North America. Taxi/rideshare to downtown is also affordable ($20-35). Unlike airports like IAD (26 miles from DC) or LAX (17 miles from downtown LA), you can reach downtown Boston from Logan in under 20 minutes by any mode.

construction2026.status: Multiple active projects across all terminals. Major capital improvement program underway.
projects: Terminal A-B Connector — APPROVED May 2024. Will link Terminals A and B post-security, eliminating need to re-clear security when transferring. Part of Massport's goal to connect all terminals post-security.
Terminal E Parking Garage — UNDER CONSTRUCTION. New 5,000-space multi-level garage with solar panels on facade and roof. Completion ~2028. Will replace currently-closed Terminal E surface lots.
Terminal B Renovation & Expansion — IN PROGRESS. Multi-year project by Turner Construction to modernize Terminal B.
Terminal C 2026 Upgrades — IN PROGRESS. Larger Gate C10 hold room, modernized security checkpoint, refreshed restrooms. New Kidport opening early 2026.
JetBlue BlueHouse BOS Lounge — OPENING 2026. 11,000 sq ft lounge in Terminal C near Gate C23. 40% larger than JetBlue's JFK lounge.
Delta One Lounge Terminal E — COMPLETED 2024. 21,000 sq ft with seafood-centric menu and 400+ seats.
Runway 9/27 EMAS Pad — COMPLETED November 2025 ($110M). Enhanced safety at runway end.
Terminal E International Expansion — COMPLETED August 2023 ($680M, 320,000 sq ft). Four new gates, distinctive red roof.
Amex Centurion Lounge — ANNOUNCED for 2029 opening. Two-story lounge with terrace in Terminal C.
construction2026.travelImpact: Terminal E surface parking closed. Terminal B renovation may cause occasional construction noise/detours. Overall airport fully operational.

tsaPreCheck.note: TSA PreCheck available at all major security checkpoints. JetBlue has expedited PreCheck lanes in Terminal C.

clear.note: CLEAR available at most security checkpoints. Particularly fast in Terminal A.

globalEntry.note: Global Entry kiosks available in Terminal E for international arrivals. Mobile Passport Control also available for U.S. citizens.

standardWaitTimes.peakWait: 30-45 minutes during peak hours and holidays
standardWaitTimes.tips: Use MyTSA app for real-time wait times. Terminal C (JetBlue) tends to have the longest waits during peak JetBlue departure banks.

arrivalRecommendation.holidays: 2.5-3 hours for Thanksgiving, Christmas, spring break

terminalA.name: Delta Sky Club (Terminal A)
terminalA.access: Delta Sky Club membership, Delta One, Delta Reserve Amex, SkyTeam Elite Plus
terminalA.amenities: Complimentary food, premium bar, WiFi, workspaces, views of airfield.

terminalA.name: Delta One Lounge
terminalA.access: Delta One passengers on qualifying international flights
terminalA.amenities: Seating for 121 guests. Elevated seafood-centric menu with 3-course meals served in under an hour. Exclusive 'living room' area. Access to adjacent Delta Sky Club.
terminalA.note: One of Delta's flagship premium lounges

terminalB.name: American Airlines Admirals Club
terminalB.location: Terminal B, upper level near Gate B4
terminalB.access: Admirals Club membership, Oneworld Emerald/Sapphire, American Airlines first/business class on qualifying flights
terminalB.amenities: Complimentary food, drinks, bar, WiFi, workspaces, shower suites.

terminalB.name: Chase Sapphire Lounge by The Club (Terminal B)
terminalB.access: Chase Sapphire Reserve cardholders, Priority Pass (limited access)
terminalB.amenities: Premium food and beverage, full bar, WiFi, workspaces, shower suites.

terminalB.name: The Lounge (Terminal B)
terminalB.access: Day pass $50 (book 24 hours in advance, subject to availability)
terminalB.amenities: Seating, food, drinks, WiFi. Budget-friendly lounge option.

terminalC.name: JetBlue BlueHouse BOS
terminalC.access: JetBlue Mosaic members, Mint class passengers
terminalC.amenities: 11,000 sq ft (40% larger than JetBlue's JFK lounge). Premium food, full bar, workspaces, WiFi. First JetBlue lounge outside NYC.

terminalC.name: United Club
terminalC.access: United Club membership, United Club Infinite card, Star Alliance Gold, United Polaris

terminalC.name: American Express Centurion Lounge (planned)
terminalC.access: Amex Platinum, Centurion, Business Platinum cardholders
terminalC.amenities: Two-story lounge with terrace, artwork by local artists, design reflecting Boston's green spaces, workstations, high-end food/beverage, high-speed WiFi.

terminalE.name: Delta Sky Club (Terminal E)
terminalE.location: Terminal E (new 21,000 sq ft lounge, opened 2024)
terminalE.access: Delta Sky Club membership, Delta One, Delta Reserve Amex, SkyTeam Elite Plus
terminalE.amenities: Seating for 400+ guests. Dedicated to international departures. Premium food, full bar, showers, WiFi, workspaces.

terminalE.name: British Airways Galleries Lounge
terminalE.location: Terminal E, 4th floor, opposite Gate E11
terminalE.access: BA First Class, Club World, Oneworld Emerald/Sapphire, Japan Airlines/Cathay Pacific premium passengers
terminalE.amenities: Complimentary food, full bar, WiFi, shower suites, workspaces.

terminalE.name: Emirates Lounge
terminalE.hours: Daily 7:05 PM - 10:05 PM (aligned with Dubai flight departure)
terminalE.access: Emirates First/Business class, Emirates Skywards Gold/Platinum
terminalE.amenities: Champagne, gourmet buffet, prayer room, showers, premium bar.

terminalE.name: Lufthansa Business Lounge
terminalE.location: Terminal E, across from Gate E11
terminalE.access: Lufthansa Business/First, Star Alliance Gold, HON Circle members
terminalE.amenities: Self-serve buffet, à la carte dining for First Class/HON Circle, full-service bar, WiFi, newspapers, TVs, shower suites.

terminalE.name: Air France Lounge
terminalE.access: Air France/KLM Business class, SkyTeam Elite Plus, Priority Pass

preSecurity.name: USO Lounge
preSecurity.access: Active-duty, Guard, Reserve, retired military, and their dependents. FREE.
preSecurity.amenities: Seating, refreshments, WiFi, charging stations, TVs, family area.

centralParking.cost: Normal parking rates apply; no additional fee for charging

terminalBGarage.cost: Normal parking rates apply; no additional fee

economyParking.cost: Normal parking rates apply; no additional fee

evCharging.totalPorts: 68 EV charging ports across all airport garages
evCharging.availability: Real-time availability via ChargePoint.com and FlyLogan app
evCharging.note: EV charging is free with standard parking — no separate electricity fee. Alternative fuel vehicles get dedicated parking spaces.

luggageStorage.onSite: Luggage storage is NOT available on-site at Logan Airport.

offSiteAlternatives.locations: Multiple locations in downtown Boston, Back Bay, Seaport District

offSiteAlternatives.note: Flexible hourly or daily. Locations near Back Bay, Faneuil Hall.

offSiteAlternatives.locations: Downtown Boston, Back Bay, North End

petFacilities.total: Multiple pet relief areas across all terminals
locations: Terminal A — pre-security and post-security pet relief areas
Terminal B — pre-security pet relief areas at both piers
Terminal C — post-security pet relief area
Terminal E — pre-security and post-security pet relief areas
petFacilities.features: Indoor relief areas with waste bags, receptacles, and clear signage

nursingRooms.note: Nursing/lactation rooms available in all terminals. Look for 'Mother's Room' or 'Nursing Room' signage. Equipped with seating, power outlets, and changing stations.

chapel.name: Our Lady of the Airways Chapel
chapel.significance: The FIRST airport chapel in the United States (opened 1951)
chapel.note: Historic. Open to travelers of all faiths.

peakTravelTimes.seasonalPeak: Summer (June-August) is peak season due to New England tourism. Fall foliage season (late Sept-mid Oct) also busy. Marathon weekend (third Monday of April), Head of the Charles Regatta (October), and Red Sox home games create traffic spikes.

arrivalRecommendation.holidays: 2.5-3 hours for Thanksgiving, Christmas/New Year, spring break, July 4th, Marathon Monday

weatherPatterns.climate: Humid continental climate with four distinct seasons. Cold snowy winters. Hot humid summers. Mild springs and falls.
weatherPatterns.commonDisruptions: Winter Nor'easters (December-March) are the single biggest disruption source — can shut down airport for 12-24 hours. Summer thunderstorms (June-August). Coastal fog in spring and fall. Occasional hurricanes (August-October).
weatherPatterns.norEastersNote: Logan is particularly vulnerable to Nor'easters — major winter storms that can dump 1-3 feet of snow and create 60+ mph winds. The airport has sophisticated snow removal but flight cancellations are common during these events. Build in 24-48 hour buffer for winter travel.
weatherPatterns.bestMonths: September through early November — mild weather, fall foliage, lower tourist crowds. Also May-June for pleasant spring conditions.
weatherPatterns.worstMonths: January-February (Nor'easters, ice storms). Late July-August (thunderstorms, humidity).
weatherPatterns.summerThunderstorms: June-August: afternoon/evening thunderstorms can cause 1-2 hour ground stops. Less disruptive than winter storms but can cascade.
weatherPatterns.winterWeather: December-March: Nor'easters are the major risk. Can shut down airport for 12-24 hours. Snow totals over 6 inches typically cause significant delays. Ice storms are particularly disruptive.
weatherPatterns.coastalFog: Spring and fall mornings can see coastal fog rolling in from the Atlantic, occasionally delaying early departures. Usually clears by mid-morning.

parkSleepFly.description: Hotel + parking + shuttle packages near Logan in East Boston, Revere, Chelsea, and Boston. Many hotels offer 7-14 days of free parking with one-night stay.
parkSleepFly.priceRange: From ~$125/night (1-night stay + up to 14 days parking + free airport shuttle)

popularHotels.name: Hilton Boston Logan Airport
popularHotels.shuttleHours: N/A — connected via sky bridge to Terminal A and E
popularHotels.highlight: The only hotel ON Logan Airport property. Connected via sky bridge.

popularHotels.name: Embassy Suites Boston at Logan Airport
popularHotels.highlight: All-suite hotel with free cooked breakfast + evening reception.

popularHotels.name: Homewood Suites Boston Logan Chelsea
popularHotels.highlight: Extended-stay hotel with kitchenettes + free breakfast.

fromSuburbanStation.caveat: Travel time from suburbs, fewer bags convenient

silverLineRoundTrip.note: Cheapest option by far. Silver Line is FREE from airport.

rideshareRoundTrip.note: Cheaper than ANY airport parking for trips of any length

savingsComparison.keyDifference: BOS offers the best price-to-convenience ratio of any major Northeast airport. The Silver Line SL1 is FREE from airport to downtown — an unbeatable deal. For travelers bringing a car, off-site lots at $17-20/day beat the $46/day Central Parking by 60-65%. Advance reservation at $29/day is a strong middle option for those who want airport-adjacent convenience. Logan Express adds a fourth option that saves suburbs travelers from driving in the city at all.

uniqueFeatures.closestMajorAirportToDowntown: At just 3 miles from downtown Boston, Logan is one of the closest major international airports to any downtown in the United States. Only NYC's LaGuardia (8 miles) and DC's Reagan (5 miles) are comparable.
uniqueFeatures.freeTransitToDowntown: The Silver Line SL1 is FREE from Logan to South Station. This is extraordinarily rare — no other major U.S. airport offers free public transit to downtown. A complete competitive advantage for BOS over every other major airport.
uniqueFeatures.firstAirportChapel: Our Lady of the Airways (1951) was the first airport chapel in the United States and inspired similar chapels at airports worldwide.
uniqueFeatures.firstRapidTransitAirport: Logan was the first major U.S. airport connected directly to a rapid transit system (Blue Line, 1952).
uniqueFeatures.firstLEEDTerminal: Terminal A was the first LEED-certified airport terminal in the United States (2005), setting a sustainability benchmark.
uniqueFeatures.peninsulaLocation: Logan sits on a peninsula in Boston Harbor, surrounded by water on three sides. Views of the Boston skyline are visible during takeoff and landing.
uniqueFeatures.jetBlueLargestFocusCity: BOS is JetBlue Airways' largest focus city globally — larger than JFK for JetBlue operations. JetBlue's BlueHouse BOS lounge opening in 2026 reflects this commitment.
uniqueFeatures.waterTransportation: Unique among major U.S. airports — passengers can arrive/depart by water taxi or ferry from the Seaport District, downtown, or suburban coastal communities. The 7-minute water taxi ride can be faster than any ground transportation during rush hour.
uniqueFeatures.bosVsJfkVsEwr: BOS is the primary New England airport and only major airport serving Greater Boston. JFK is 220 miles south. BOS's proximity to downtown (3 miles) and free Silver Line to South Station make it superior to both JFK and EWR for downtown-bound travelers.
uniqueFeatures.runwayLayout: 6 runways arranged in a near-triangle — unusual for a major airport. Allows simultaneous takeoffs/landings in multiple wind conditions. Runway 15L/33R is only 2,557 ft — used only by small aircraft.
uniqueFeatures.nor'easterVulnerability: Logan is notoriously vulnerable to winter Nor'easters. The airport's snow removal is sophisticated but the combination of coastal location, Atlantic moisture, and cold temperatures creates unpredictable conditions.
uniqueFeatures.noPerimeterRule: Unlike DCA, Logan has no perimeter rule or slot controls. Airlines can fly any distance. Major international gateway for New England.

emergencyContacts.flyLoganApp: Download FlyLogan app for flight status, parking reservations, Mark My Car feature
